question: What is the date and the time of the message suggesting that Biden seems like a morning person?
relevant_doc:
(Ooops, accidentally hit Enter too early and posted that before I was done.  To continue:)

Factors that I think went into Biden’s terrible debate performance:
* He had a cold.  (This was my assumption from his first few lines, and it was confirmed after the debate.)  He clearly should have mentioned that explicitly early on; it would have helped.
* He seems to be a morning person, and Trump seems to be a night owl (witness the tweets at 3:00am when he was president)
* He clearly was trying to squeeze in a bunch of facts and figures (and sometimes getting them wrong) in a short period of time, which often meant he wasted most of his time on stuff that didn’t emotionally connect with his audience.  Trump, by contrast, took the emotional lies he wanted to tell and ran with the same ones over and over.  This was a failure of Biden’s debate prep.
* As is common knowledge, Biden has always had a stutter.  That’s going to be made harder to deal with by illness or fatigue.

None of that excuses Biden’s poor performance; better debate prep (which might mean *less* debate prep!) would have helped a lot with all of that.  But it does mean that the Biden we saw on stage last night is not the only Biden American voters are going to see between now and the election.

Did last night put a smile on Putin’s, Orbán’s, Xi’s, and Khamenei’s faces?  I’m sure it did.  But I don’t think it changed the tragectory of the 2024 US presidential election very much (and some of the tracking polls suggest that it might even, bizarre as this seems, have changed it in Biden’s direction, although I suspect that’s just the direction voter sentiment was already moving in, rather than a consequence of the election).

So I’m disappointed, but still pretty hopeful.  I think Biden’s chances in the election are better than Trump’s (even if they may not be as *much* better as they were on Wednesday).

(As for the idea of replacing Biden with a different candidate, that sounds great if the perfect vetted candidate existed and could unify the Democratic party behind them, but that candidate doesn’t exist and there would be a huge fight over who got to take up the mantle, with the effect that Democrats would still be bitterly divided in November, while Repbublicans had been united behind their candidate for months.  It would be different in a parliamentary system, or a similar system where parties choose their leaders/standardbearers without a primary process, but in the US system as it has evolved it would be a mess, and I don’t think there’s any way the resulting Democratic candidate would be in a better position to win the general election than Biden is now.)
